title: Talent & Learning Manager (Fixed Term Contract)
description:
The Opportunity…

At Moët Hennessy, We Don't Just Create Exceptional Experiences – We Live Them.

For generations, our 27 Maisons, including Dom Pérignon, Veuve Clicquot, and Hennessy, have defined luxury. Now, it's your turn. Imagine a career fueled by passion and creativity, where you redefine what's possible. We seek individuals who thrive in collaborative environments, sharing our love for craftsmanship, elegance, and integrity. We celebrate diversity and the pursuit of excellence.

Ready to drive learning and talent innovation at a global luxury brand? As Talent and Learning Manager for Moët Hennessy in Asia Pacific, your role will involve partnering with regional and global teams to design and implement strategic L&D initiatives. Your work will directly empower our people, build essential capabilities, and cultivate an engaging and inclusive culture that is critical to our ongoing success. If you're passionate about fostering continuous growth, enhancing critical skills, and nurturing an engaging work environment, this is your opportunity to contribute to our success.

This is a 9-momth fixed-term contract.

How you will contribute…

Learning & Development Strategy and Execution

  • Strategic L&D Leadership: Support the development and implementation of the regional Talent & Learning strategy, aligning with regional and global objectives and leverage existing tools and platforms to foster a culture of continuous learning and critical capability enhancement across Asia Pacific.
  • Capability & Needs Assessment: Conduct thorough skills assessments and needs analyses to identify critical skill gaps and development opportunities, directly informing the design of targeted training solutions that support the regional SOAR strategy.
  • Program Design & Delivery: Lead the end-to-end design, development, and facilitation of a diverse learning calendar, encompassing engaging workshops, digital learning, and experiential programs, ensuring high-quality content delivery.
  • Stakeholder Collaboration & Curation: Act as a strong people partner, collaborating effectively with global and regional teams, and engaging with external vendors to curate and deliver high-quality, relevant learning content.
  • Performance Measurement & Improvement: Establish and implement robust evaluation methods for all learning programs and initiatives, utilizing data and feedback to drive continuous improvement and demonstrate impact.
  • Learning Culture & Engagement: Champion a dynamic, feedback-driven learning culture, actively promoting participative learning and growth initiatives throughout the organization.

Talent Development & Management:

  • Strategic Talent Advancement: Drive regional talent investment strategies and programs, focusing on upskilling, leadership advancement, and overall capability building to support the long-term talent agenda.
  • Talent Pipeline & Mobility: Collaborate with HR teams to actively build a robust and diverse talent pipeline within the region, ensuring talent mobility and readiness for future organizational needs.
  • High-Potential Development: Identify and nurture high-potential talent, providing targeted development opportunities to accelerate their growth and prepare them for future leadership roles.

Other:

  • Budget Management & Operational Excellence: Manage the L&D and Talent Development budget effectively, ensuring efficient allocation of resources, and overseeing administrative processes to support the smooth execution of all programs and initiatives.
  • Cross-Functional Support & Ad-Hoc Initiatives: Provide responsive support for various HR and business initiatives, including contributing to ad-hoc projects, preparing reports, and handling other duties as assigned to advance organizational talent objectives


We are looking for unique and amazing talent. Join us if you have…

  • 8+ years of experience in Learning & Development, Talent Management, or a similar HR function, with a strong focus on training and program delivery.
  • Proven experience in designing, implementing, and evaluating learning programs with experience using LMS platforms
  • Strong understanding of adult learning principles and instructional design methodologies.
  • Excellent communication, presentation, and facilitation skills.
  • Ability to work effectively in a multicultural and fast-paced environment.
  • Proactive, results-oriented, and a strong team player.
  • Proficiency in English; additional regional languages are a plus


When you join Moët Hennessy…

  • You embark on a career journey with the leaders of luxury wines, champagnes, and spirits, within the prestigious LVMH Group.
  • You enter a world of possibility where career development opportunities across our 5 global regions and within the entire LVMH Group are unmatched.
  • You join a passionate workforce within our internationally diverse and inclusive teams, exploring the rich heritage of our Maisons and shaping the next chapter of our story
  • You discover our essential relationship with our terroirs and the soils that define our products. Through our Living Soils Living Together program, we are committed to regenerating soils, reducing climate impact, and empowering communities for a sustainable future.
  • You will share in our passion for hosting, the art of living, and creating products that celebrate life’s most memorable milestones, always experienced through responsible tasting.

Ready for a world of possibilities? Discover Moët Hennessy today!

postedCompany.name: MOET HENNESSY ASIA PACIFIC PTE. LTD.
